sections.paragraph:
παραινέω impf. παρῄνουν; aor. παρῄνεσα LXX, ptc. παραινέσας LXX (Pind., Hdt. et al.; ins, pap, LXX, Philo, Joseph.; TestGad 6:1) to advise strongly, recommend, urge τινά (instead of the st. dat.; s. B-D-F §152, 3; Rob. 475; and s. Ps.-Callisth. 3, 4, 16 παρῄνουν τὸν Ἀλέξανδρον οἱ Μακεδόνες) w. inf. foll. (Jos., Bell. 5, 87f, Ant. 1, 201.—B-D-F §392, 1d; 409, 5; Rdm.2 121; 226) Ac 27:22. Abs. (SIG 89, 42) w. direct disc. foll. IMg 6:1; foll. by λέγων αὐτοῖς and direct disc. Ac 27:9 (on the impf. παρῄνει s. B-D-F §328). τινί τι recommend someth. to someone (Oenomaus in Eus., PE 5, 25, 1; Philo, Poster. Cai. 13) ISm 4:1. τὶ (Chion, Ep. 16, 1 παρῄνουν ταῦτα) Lk 3:18 D.—KWeidinger, Die Haustafeln: Ein Stück urchristlicher Paränese, 1928.—DELG s.v. αἶνος. M-M.

impf. impf. = imperfect

aor. aor. = aorist

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

ptc. ptc. = participle

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

TestGad TestGad = Testament of Gad, s. Test12Patr—List 2

dat. dat. = dative

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

Ps.-Callisth Ps.-Callisth , Historia Alexandri Magni, II–III? a.d.—Lists 5

inf. inf. = infinitive

foll. foll. = followed, following

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Rdm. Rdm. = LRadermacher, Neutestamentliche Grammatik—List 6

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

IMg IMg = Ignatius to the Magnesians—List 1

someth. someth. = something

Oenomaus Oenomaus , II a.d.—List 5

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

ISm ISm = Ignatius to the Smyrnaeans, I–II a.d.—List 1

Chion Chion , IV b.c.; for letters attributed to him, I a.d., s. Ep.—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 764
